ocr.text: The analysis of test–retest reliability and internal consistency reveals the following: for Total, the 1st mean (M) and standard
deviation (SD) are 109.4 (8.75) and 105.4 (9.55), while the 2nd M and SD are 109.6 (8.12) and 106.2 (8.54), with ta values of
−0.20 and −0.79, pa values of 0.845 and 0.432, and Cronbach α values of 0.817 and 0.806. For Social skills, the 1st M and SD
are 23.3 (4.48) and 21.7 (4.16), with 2nd M and SD of 23.4 (4.06) and 21.7 (3.63), ta values of −0.27 and −0.15, pa of 0.784
and 0.883, and Cronbach α of 0.743 and 0.760. Attention switching shows 1st M and SD of 24.1 (2.93) and 23.4 (2.98), with
2nd M and SD of 23.7 (2.51) and 23.2 (2.56), ta of 1.15 and 0.70, pa of 0.250 and 0.485, and Cronbach α of 0.640 and 0.657.
Attention to detail has 1st M and SD of 22.5 (4.00) and 21.9 (4.19), with 2nd M and SD of 22.6 (3.71) and 22.0 (3.75), ta of
−0.18 and −0.26, pa of 0.857 and 0.792, and Cronbach α of 0.673 and 0.694. For Communication, the 1st M and SD are 20.1
Cronbach α of 0.765 and 0.756. Lastly, Imagination shows 1st M and SD of 19.3 (3.01) and 19.1 (3.38), with 2nd M and SD of
19.5 (2.87) and 19.4 (3.22), ta of −0.54 and −0.91, pa of 0.588 and 0.366, and Cronbach α of 0.622 and 0.619. In Table 2, the
run lengths indicate that for a run length of 1, the all experiments total is 44, with a winning streak length of 1, and results for
Experiment 1, Experiment 2, and Experiment 3 each show 23 (±2). For a run length of 2, the all experiments total is 24,
winning streak length is 2, with Experiment 1 showing 11 (±2), Experiment 2 showing 12 (±2), and Experiment 3 showing 11
(±2). For run length 3, the all experiments total is 9, with a winning streak length of 3, and Experiment 1, Experiment 2, and
Experiment 3 yielding results of 6 (±2), 6 (±2), and 5 (±2), respectively. Run length 4 has an all experiments total of 7, with a
winning streak length of 4, and results of 3 (±1) across all three experiments. For run length 5, the all experiments total is 5,
with a winning streak length greater than 4, and results for Experiment 1, Experiment 2, and Experiment 3 showing 2 (±2), 2
(±2), and 2 (±2), while the losing streaks show >4 for all three experiments, with streak lengths of 3 (±2), 2 (±2), and 1 (±2).
